summary: IonQ is a direct provider platform. Its API returns results as a normalized probability histogram rather than raw shot counts, which is preserved verbatim by the adapter.

fixture_to_public_schema.snapshot.status: IonQ job status (submitted/ready/running/completed/failed/canceled); mapped to canonical COMPLETED here.
fixture_to_public_schema.result.raw_payload.histogram: IonQ probability histogram keyed by integer state index.

provenance_semantics.results_evidence: measurement_probabilities are provider_supplied; estimated_measurement_counts are derived (independent per-bucket rounding of probabilities x shots). measurement_counts are never fabricated because IonQ does not return raw counts.
provenance_semantics.note: This asymmetry (probabilities, not counts) is the principal IonQ-specific distinction the unified model captures without loss.
